title: Mock Exam H
description: A 22 task RHCSA style mock exam covering repositories, SELinux HTTP changes, chrony, package work, and container inspection.

tasks:
Configure networking on clientvm with the following settings:

IP ADDRESS: 192.168.122.40
NETMASK: 255.255.255.0
GATEWAY: 192.168.122.1
DNS SERVER: 192.168.122.3
HOSTNAME: clientvm.exam-h.lab
Add a persistent hosts entry so registry.exam-h.lab resolves to 192.168.122.3.
Configure a repository file on clientvm with BaseOS and AppStream served from servervm, enabled, and with gpgcheck disabled.
Configure the same repository file on servervm.

Configure Apache on clientvm so it serves the existing site on TCP port 8181.

Requirements:
- Start automatically at boot.
- Open the port permanently in the firewall.
- Apply the SELinux change required for the new port.
Configure pwquality so passwords require a minimum length of 12 and at least 3 character classes.
Create user agingh without a home directory, with shell /sbin/nologin, and set its password to cinder9.
Set password aging for agingh to minimum 2 days, maximum 30 days, warning 7 days, and force a password change at the next login.
Create /srv/silver-drop as a sticky directory with ownership root:root and mode 1777.
Configure chronyd on servervm so it serves time to 192.168.122.0/24 and starts automatically at boot.
Configure chronyd on clientvm so it synchronizes only with servervm and starts automatically at boot.
On clientvm, add a permanent rich firewall rule allowing TCP port 2222 only from 192.168.122.0/24.
Set the default inactive period for newly created local users to 10 days.
Find all files under /opt/exam-h/find that are owned by watcherh and were modified within the last 24 hours, then copy them to /root/watcherh-files while preserving the source directory structure.
Extract lines containing silver from /usr/share/dict/words into /root/silver-lines.
Create /root/usr-local-h.tar.gz containing /usr/local.
On /dev/sdb, create a 672 MiB swap partition and configure it persistently.
Resize /dev/reviewvgh/reviewh so the final size is 320 MiB without losing the existing file system or data.
Configure clientvm to boot into multi-user.target by default. Ensure rsyslog is enabled and running. If postfix is installed, disable it and stop it.
Use the prepared local repositories to install the packages tree and dos2unix on clientvm. Remove dos2unix and leave tree installed.

Create user inspecth with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. As that user, load /opt/rhcsa/container-assets/rhcsa-httpd-base.tar into local storage and write the configured working directory of localhost/rhcsa-httpd-base:latest to /home/inspecth/workdir.txt.
Apply the recommended tuned profile and leave it active.
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.40/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
firewall-cmd --permanent --add-rich-rule='rule family="ipv4" source address="192.168.122.0/24" port protocol="tcp" port="2222" accept'
# Add the swap entry with the UUID reported above
checks: hostnamectl --static | grep -qx 'clientvm.exam-h.lab' && grep -Fqx '192.168.122.3 registry.exam-h.lab' /etc/hosts && curl -fsS http://servervm/repo/BaseOS/repodata/repomd.xml >/dev/null && ssh admin@servervm sudo curl -fsS http://servervm/repo/AppStream/repodata/repomd.xml >/dev/null
curl -fsS http://localhost:8181 >/dev/null && semanage port -l | grep -Eq '^http_port_t\b.*\b8181\b' && firewall-cmd --list-rich-rules | grep -Fq 'port port="2222" protocol="tcp" accept'

grep -Eq '^minlen\s*=\s*12$' /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/silverpeak.conf && grep -Eq '^minclass\s*=\s*3$' /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/silverpeak.conf && getent passwd agingh | awk -F: '{print $6":"$7}' | grep -qx ':/sbin/nologin' && chage -l agingh | grep -Eq 'Minimum.*2' && chage -l agingh | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*30' && chage -l agingh | grep -Eq 'warning.*7' && chage -l agingh | grep -Eq 'password must be changed|must be changed' && useradd -D | grep -Eq 'INACTIVE=10' && stat -c '%a %U:%G' /srv/silver-drop | grep -qx '1777 root:root'

grep -Eq '^server servervm iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && ssh admin@servervm sudo grep -Eq '^allow 192\.168\.122\.0/24$' /etc/chrony.conf && ssh admin@servervm sudo systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled

test -f /root/watcherh-files/opt/exam-h/find/a/file1.txt && grep -q 'silver' /root/silver-lines && test -f /root/usr-local-h.tar.gz && swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx '/dev/sdb1' && lvs --noheadings -o lv_name,vg_name,lv_size --units m --nosuffix | awk '$1=="reviewh" && $2=="reviewvgh" && $3>=319 && $3<=321{f=1} END{exit !f}' && systemctl get-default | grep -qx multi-user.target && systemctl is-enabled rsyslog | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-enabled postfix | grep -qx disabled && rpm -q tree >/dev/null && ! rpm -q dos2unix >/dev/null 2>&1

runuser -l inspecth -c 'podman image exists localhost/rhcsa-httpd-base:latest' && test -s /home/inspecth/workdir.txt && rec="$(tuned-adm recommend | awk '{print $1}')"; act="$(tuned-adm active | sed -E 's/.*: ([^ ]+).*/\1/')"; test -n "$rec" && test "$act" = "$rec"
